title: Shattering Bitter Mexican Scamorza Flan
description: A festive Mexican dessert from the Yucatecan region featuring scamorza cheese, perfect for a crowd. The combination of bitter and sweet flavors will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Knead 250g scamorza cheese into a fine crumb using a stand mixer or by hand for 5 minutes, until well combined.
Transfer the cheese crumb to a stand mixer and add 150g coconut sugar, 400ml coconut milk, 2 tablespoons lime juice, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon. Mix well until combined.
Add 3 egg yolks and continue mixing until smooth and free of lumps.
Pour the mixture into 6 ramekins or small cups and bake at 375F (190C) for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are slightly jiggly.
Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
To serve, dip each flan into hot water for 10-15 seconds, then invert onto plates. The cheese should shatter and the center should be set.
Top with dark chocolate chips, if desired.
